response.justification: Sarah Smith's resume shows strong generalist software engineering skills, distributed systems experience, and hands-on work with agentic AI infrastructure (e.g., MCP code generators at re:cinq). However, her background lacks direct experience with fintech infrastructure (credit systems, payment flows, embedded finance) or regulatory compliance, which are core requirements. Her leadership experience is limited to smaller teams (10-20 people) and does not demonstrate scaling from 10 to 30+ engineers. While she has event-driven system experience (GISCollective, Optilyz), it is not explicitly tied to agentic AI or high-stakes financial operations.
strengths: Strong distributed-systems experience with event-driven architectures (e.g., GISCollective, Dunnhumby)
Hands-on work with agentic AI infrastructure (MCP, developer tooling at re:cinq)
Proven ability to work across tech stacks (TypeScript, D, Node.js, React, etc.)
concerns: No demonstrated experience in fintech infrastructure (credit systems, payments, or embedded finance)
Limited leadership scale-up experience (no clear track record scaling teams to 30+ engineers)
Lacks direct experience operating AI systems in regulated, high-stakes commercial environments
